evals.prompt: The Pencil desktop app is closed and I can't connect to the MCP. I need to quickly find all frames related to 'settings' in our design file at royalti-client-2.5/pencil/royalti-client.pen. What settings screens exist, and how many are there?
evals.expected_output: Lists all settings-related frames found via binary parsing. Should use offline mode scripts, not attempt MCP connection. Should check for sidecar map first.

assertions.text: Uses binary parser script or reads .map.md — does NOT attempt Pencil MCP connection
Correctly identifies the settings screens (9.1 through 9.10 plus mobile variants)
Reports approximately 10-11 unique settings screens (9.1-9.10 desktop + some mobile)
Output is organized/grouped, not a raw flat dump of all frame names

evals.prompt: We have a Pencil design file at royalti-client-2.5/pencil/royalti-client.pen. I want to make sure we have a proper sidecar map for it so future sessions are fast. Can you check if a map exists and generate or update it as needed? The Pencil MCP is connected.
evals.expected_output: Checks for existing map file, finds it already exists, confirms or verifies it looks complete. Should NOT blindly regenerate from scratch.

assertions.text: Checks the pencil/ directory for an existing .map.md before attempting to generate
Correctly identifies that royalti-client.map.md already exists
Recommends verifying/updating the existing map rather than regenerating from scratch
Mentions get_editor_state as the correct MCP-based generation method

evals.prompt: I'm about to add a new Webhooks settings screen to our Pencil design file (royalti-client-2.5/pencil/royalti-client.pen). It would be screen 9.11. Before I start, can you check what settings screens already exist and give me the IDs I'll need to reference nearby frames?
evals.expected_output: Reads the sidecar map file to get settings frame IDs. Does NOT do a broad MCP query. Returns correct IDs for 9.x frames from the map.

assertions.text: Reads royalti-client.map.md (not the .pen binary or a broad MCP query)
Does not suggest or perform a broad batch_get with patterns on the full document
Lists settings frames (9.x) with their correct node IDs from the map
Identifies 9.10 (Settings Delivery, ID: ECuDu) as the last existing settings screen before 9.11

evals.prompt: I've just started working on the nso-to project. There's a Pencil design file at nso-to/pencil/nso-to.pen. Can you set up the sidecar map for it so we can work with it efficiently? The Pencil MCP is NOT connected — use offline mode.
evals.expected_output: Parses nso-to.pen offline, creates sidecar map at nso-to.map.md (not nso-to.pen.map.md), includes frame IDs, organized by phase.

assertions.text: Uses offline binary parsing (no Pencil MCP connection attempted)
Creates map at correct sidecar path: nso-to.map.md (stem-only, no .pen in name)
Map includes node IDs for each frame (not just names)
Map is organized by phase/section (not a flat dump)

evals.prompt: I have an existing sidecar map for royalti-client-2.5/pencil/royalti-client.pen. The map header says 'Total frames: 155' and 'Total reusable components: 77'. The Pencil MCP is connected. Before I start designing, can you verify the map is still accurate?
evals.expected_output: Runs drift check by calling get_editor_state and comparing counts against map header. If counts differ, identifies the delta and updates the map before proceeding.

assertions.text: Reads the .map.md file first to get the stated counts (155 frames, 77 components)
Calls get_editor_state({ include_schema: false }) to get current MCP counts
Explicitly compares the map counts against the MCP-reported counts
If counts differ, identifies which frames were added/removed and updates the map before proceeding

evals.prompt: I just used batch_design to add a new frame '9.11 — Settings Webhooks' to royalti-client-2.5/pencil/royalti-client.pen. The operation binding returned the new node ID 'XyZ99'. Can you update the sidecar map to reflect this addition?
evals.expected_output: Updates the sidecar map: adds the new frame row in the correct section (Settings, section 9), increments the total frame count in the header.

assertions.text: Reads the current royalti-client.map.md before making changes
Adds '| XyZ99 | 9.11 — Settings Webhooks |' to the Settings section (section 9)
Increments the 'Total frames' count in the map header by 1
Does not modify or remove any existing rows in the map

evals.prompt: I'm trying to work with royalti-client-2.5/pencil/royalti-client.pen but the Pencil MCP keeps dropping — get_editor_state times out. I need to find all frames in section 5 (Releases) and their IDs. Can you help?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes MCP is unavailable, falls back to offline mode. Reads the sidecar map (preferred) or uses binary parsing to find section 5 frames. Does not repeatedly retry failing MCP calls.

assertions.text: Acknowledges the MCP connection issue rather than repeatedly retrying
Switches to offline mode — reads .map.md or uses binary parser scripts
Checks for and reads the sidecar map first (fastest offline path)
Returns the section 5 (Releases) frames with their IDs from the map

evals.prompt: I have a design file at nso-to/pencil/nso-to.pen. When I look in the pencil directory, I see both 'nso-to.map.md' and 'nso-to.pen.map.md'. Which one is correct and what should I do about the duplicate?
evals.expected_output: Identifies nso-to.map.md as the correct name (stem-only rule). Flags nso-to.pen.map.md as incorrectly named. Recommends deleting or merging the incorrect one.

assertions.text: Cites the sidecar naming rule: strip .pen extension, use stem only
Identifies nso-to.map.md as the correctly named file
Flags nso-to.pen.map.md as incorrectly named (has .pen in the filename)
Recommends deleting the incorrect file (after checking if it has unique content worth merging)
